syntax_2985.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2985.title: On Log
syntax_2985.description: Called when a message is logged.

en: Called when a message is logged.

syntax_2986.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2986.title: Log Message
syntax_2986.description: Logs a message with the given log level. The last pattern uses 'info' level.
syntax_2986.syntax_pattern:
(log|print [in]) %loglevel% [[with [the]] message[s]] %strings%
send %loglevel% [[with [the]] message[s]] %strings% to [the] console
print [in] %strings%

en: Logs a message with the given log level. The last pattern uses 'info' level.

syntax_2987.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2987.title: Log Level
syntax_2987.description: Returns the level of the message in a log event.

en: Returns the level of the message in a log event.

syntax_2989.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2989.title: Logger Name
syntax_2989.description:
Returns the name of the logger in a log event. None if the logger name doesn't exist.
If a plugin logs a message using its plugin logger this will be name of the plugin. i.e. '[<Plugin>] <message>'.
In this case the logged message won't contain this '[Plugin]' prefix. So you can check if the logger name is a valid plugin somehow and merge it with the logged message.

en:
Returns the name of the logger in a log event. None if the logger name doesn't exist.
If a plugin logs a message using its plugin logger this will be name of the plugin. i.e. '[<Plugin>] <message>'.
In this case the logged message won't contain this '[Plugin]' prefix. So you can check if the logger name is a valid plugin somehow and merge it with the logged message.

syntax_2990.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2990.title: Log Level
syntax_2990.type_usage: info, warning, error, fatal, trace, debug, off, all

syntax_2988.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2988.title: Logged Message
syntax_2988.description: Returns the logged message in a log event.

en: Returns the logged message in a log event.
